coredata.dc:title: Synthesis, characterization and cells and tissues imaging of carbon quantum dots
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Jing
Li, Qilong
Zhou, JingE.
Wang, Yiting
Yu, Lei
Peng, Hui
Zhu, Jianzhong
coredata.dc:description: Compare to other quantum dots, carbon quantum dots have its own incomparable advantages, such as low cell toxicity, favorable biocompatibility, cheap production cost, mild reaction conditions, easy to large-scale synthesis and functionalization. In this thesis, we took citric acid monohydrate and diethylene glycol bis (3-aMinopropyl) ether as materials, used decomposition method to acquire carbon quantum dots (CQDs) which can emission blue fluorescence under ultraviolet excitation. In the aspect of application, we achieved the biological imaging of CQDs in vivo and in vitro.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon quantum dots
Bio-imaging
Citric acid monohydrate
Diethylene Glycol Bis (3-aminopropyl) Ether

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Absorption and emission of the CQDs
Cytotoxicity in vitro of the CQDs

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TEM image (HRTEM inset) of the CQDs prepared.
Diameter distribution histograms of the CQDs prepared.
XRD pattern for the QCDs prepared.
The ultraviolet absorption spectrum of the CQDs at different concentrations 1) 340, 2) 85, 3) 21.3, 4) 1.33 and 5) 0.33 mM. The peaks of absorption is about 200 nm.

Fluorescence emission spectra of the CQDs at different wavelength excitation: 1) 300, 2) 320, 3) 340, 4) 360, 380 and 6) 400 nm. PL intensity increase with decrease of excitation wavelength and the of the fluorescence emission peaks move slightly toward violet direction. Inset: normalized emission spectra. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)
Dependence of survival rate for NCI-H460 cell on concentration of the CQDs.
Fluorescence images of CQDs for NCI-H460 cells.
Fluorescence images of the tissue section for kidney (A) and Liver (B). 1) glomeruli, 2) kidney tubules, 3) renal veins, 4) hepatic vein, and 5) Liver cell nucleus.

head.ce:title: Synthesis, characterization and cells and tissues imaging of carbon quantum dots

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering, East China Normal University, Shanghai, 200062, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Molecular Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Compare to other quantum dots, carbon quantum dots have its own incomparable advantages, such as low cell toxicity, favorable biocompatibility, cheap production cost, mild reaction conditions, easy to large-scale synthesis and functionalization. In this thesis, we took citric acid monohydrate and diethylene glycol bis (3-aMinopropyl) ether as materials, used decomposition method to acquire carbon quantum dots (CQDs) which can emission blue fluorescence under ultraviolet excitation. In the aspect of application, we achieved the biological imaging of CQDs in vivo and in vitro.

TEM image (HRTEM inset) of the CQDs prepared.
Image 1

ce:section.ce:para: During the past several years, fluorescent semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) have attracted much interest due to their unique optical and biochemical properties and their significant practical application in many fields such as biomedical imaging, optoelectronics devices, biological labelling and sensing etc. [1–3] However, such QDs suffer from some serious health and environmental concerns due to the use of heavy metals as the essential elements, which limits their wide application [4–6]. As a result, considerable attention has recently been paid to developing new nanomaterials with similar optical properties [7]. Fluorescent carbon dots (CDs) exhibit unique properties including low toxicity, easy labelling, and highly stability, and have been widely used for bio-imaging and optical imaging [8–11]. The preparative methods for CDs have been well-developed so far, including laser ablation of graphite or carbon powder [12,13], carbonizing polymerized resols on silica spheres [14,15], electrochemical release or exfoliation from a graphite source [16–21], chemical oxidation of activation carbon or graphite [22,23], separation of combusted carbon soot [24–26], thermal oxidation of suitable molecular precursors [27–29], dehydration of carbohydrates [30,31], hydrothermal routes for cutting graphene sheets [32], proton-beam irradiation of nano-diamonds [33,34], pyrolysis of suitable molecular precursors [35,36] etc.

Hydrothermal method is one of the most common method of preparation of CDs due to its simple synthetic process and more uniform particle size, which is favored more and more by researchers. The research emphasis of this method is to try and choose different carbon source, to find the best choice. In this study, citric acid monohydrate and diethylene glycol bis (3-aminopropyl) ether were used as new precursors for the one-step carbonization synthesis of CQDs. They were successfully applied to cells and organization imaging in vitro and in vivo completing the biological evaluation of CQDs.

Citric acid monohydrate (C6H8O7·H2O, purity ≥ 99.5%) was purchased from chemical reagent Co., LTD, China national medicine group. Diethylene Glycol Bis(3-aminopropyl) Ether (C10H24N2O3, purity≥96%) was purchased from Tokyo Kasei Kogyo Co., LTD. Cell Counting Kit-8(CCK-8) was purchased from Dojindo China Co., LTD. DMEM (Cell culture medium, high glucose, 1% non-essential amino acids and l - glutamine, 110 mg/L of sodium pyruvate), Hank's Balanced Salt Solution (HBSS, Does not contain calcium and magnesium ions), Phosphate Buffer Solution (PBS), 0.25% of trypsin (Does not contain EDTA) and Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) was purchased from Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (Gibco). Fetal bovine serum (FBS) was purchased from Biological Industries (BioInd), Penicillin-Streptomycin Solution was purchased from HyClone. Propranolol hydrochloride (purity ≥ 95%) was purchased from Shanghai Vita Chemical Reagent Co LTD. All reagents were of analytical grade or the highest purity available. Milli-Q water was used throughout the experiments.
The NCI- H460 cell lines (Caco-2 cell line) (40–60 generation) was purchased from Shanghai Labpal Co. LTD.

Kunming mice (weighing 18–22 g, age 8 weeks) was obtained from the Experimental Animal Center of Medical School, Shanghai Jiao Tong University. All procedures of animal experiments complied with the regulations of the ‘‘protection of animals used for experimental and other scientific purposes’’ from the relevant directives of Good Laboratory Practices (GLP). The study protocols were approved by the Ethical Committee at our institutes.

TEM and XRD images were recorded by a JEOL JSM-6700F Electron Microscope (Japan), HRTEM (Hitachi H-9000) and a D/MAX 2550V with Cu Kα radiation, respectively. Cell counter and Millicell-ERS volt-ohmmeter (Merck Millipore), Ultraviolet spectrophotometer, Fluorescence spectrophotometer and high performance liquid phase chromatography (HPLC) (shimadzu co., LTD, Japan), laser particle analyzer, mastersizer2000 (Malvern Instrument LTD), Freezing microtome (Leica), Multiskan MK3 Automatic enzyme standard instrument and the cell constant temperature incubator (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for relevant test.

A typical synthesis consists of heating 4.32 g citric acid monohydrate and 9 ml Diethylene Glycol BIS (3-aminopropyl) Ether in a three neck flask at 400 °C for 2 h under vigorous magnetic stirring and nitrogen protection to yield CDs. When the drops of water slowly appear on wall of the flask from room temperature to 80 °C, start pumping out of water, to ensure the reaction according to the positive direction. After 20 min, the color of the solution turned from colorless to yellow, reddish brown final black. After cooling, a little of water was added into the flask, which was centrifuged at 4 °C, 8000 revolutions per minute for 5 min. Repeated the above steps 3 times, supernatant fluid until no precipitation. The supernatant fluid was transferred in a dialysis bag with 500 molecular intercept to dialysis for 7 days. The CQDs solution was diluted to 50 ml by water to form a stock solution for stepwise dilution.

100 μL density of 4 × 105/ml NCI- H460 cells suspension in the 96 - well plate was cultivated in an incubator with a 5% CO2 atmosphere at 37 °C for 24 h. As cytotoxicity test samples, 20 μL CQDs solutions with 7 different concentrations were added into 96 – wells respectively. In addition, two wells without CQDs were as blank and negative control, respectively. After the 96 - well plate was cultivated for 48 h and then the solution was discarded, 100 μL of new culture medium and 10 μL solution of CCK-8 were added into wells. The 96 - well plate was cultivated for 4 h. The absorbance values of the solution in the wells were measured at 490 nm by a MK3 enzyme standard instrument. The cell survival rate were calculated as follows:
(1) Cell survival rate (%) = [Awith CQDs-ABlank]/[Awithout CQDs-ABlank] × 100%
where, Awith CQDs is the absorbance value of the solution in the well with NCI- H460 cells, CCK-8 solution and CQDs, ABlank the absorbance value of the solution in the well with culture medium, and CCK-8 solution, Awithout CQDs the absorbance value of the solution in the well with NCI- H460 cells, CCK-8 solution.

The medium should be fully washed out with HBSS in order to avoid fluorescence imaging being interference by the medium. The different concentration of carbon quantum dots (HBSS solution) was cultured into the incubator for 30 min (too long cells can't stick wall, influence the results) and the carbon quantum dots solution was suck out. After the petri dish was washed with HBSS, the fluorescence imaging of the cells was observed by fluorescence microscope at 400 nm of excitation wavelength.

One ml of appropriate concentration of CQDs solution was poured into the stomach of the kunming mice 3 times every day. Three days later, the kidney and liver were taken out and embedded into frozen section embedding medium for 1 h which was rapidly formed into solid block by using liquid nitrogen. The block was frozen in a refrigerator at - 30 °C for 1 h. The next day, the block was sliced up into 6 μm – tick section on a slide with frozen section machine and observed using a fluorescence microscope.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Absorption and emission of the CQDs
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 4 shows the ultraviolet absorption spectrum of the CQDs at different concentrations 1) 340, 2) 85, 3) 21.3, 4) 1.33 and 5) 0.33 mM. It is seen that the UV–vis spectrum shows a strong absorption peak at 230 nm. Fig. 5 shows the PL emission spectra of the CQDs dispersion with various excitation wavelengths from 300 to 400 nm. Note that a strong PL emission spectrum centered at 440 nm was observed when it was excited at 300 nm, indicating the production of fluorescent CQDs. The image of the dispersion under UV light (300 nm) exhibits an obvious blue color, further revealing that the resultant CQDs exhibit strong blue fluorescence.

It is seen that the emission spectrum of the CQDs thus obtained changes from 440 nm to 480 nm, with a dependence on the excitation wavelengths from 300 to 400 nm. The emission peak position shifts to longer wavelengths and the intensity gradually decreases with increased excitation wavelengths. Such observation is similar to that of carbon dots, which may be attributed to the optical selection of differently sized nanoparticles (quantum effect) or different emissive traps on the CNDs-R surface or another mechanism altogether. The full width at half-maximum (FWHM) at 300 nm is about 80 nm, indicating the narrow size distribution of the CQDs size. Furthermore, the quantum yield of CQDs is 11%.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Cytotoxicity in vitro of the CQDs
ce:section.ce:para: The testing results of cytotoxicity in vitro were shown in Fig. 6 . With the increasing of the concentration of CQDs from 0.083 mM to 340.0 mM, the cell survival rate relaxedly decreased from 100% to 95% followed decreased rapidly to 88% ≥ 680 mM of the concentration for CQDs. This suggests that the CQDs exhibits very low toxicity to cells in vitro when the concentration is lower than 340.0 mM.

The fluorescence imaging of CQDs for NCI-H460 cell shows in Fig. 7 . There are the bright blue fluorescence in the cell grew area while the dark in the area no cell, reflecting the tendency of CQDs gathering toward cell.

Fluorescence images of the tissue section for kidney and Liver were shown in Fig. 8 (A) and (B), respectively. Big differences between them is the result of the organizational structures of the two organs are very different. In Fig. 8, arrow1 is glomeruli, arrow 2 kidney tubules, arrow 3 renal veins, arrow 4 hepatic vein, and arrow 5 liver cell nucleus. It explained that the CQDs can be uptaken by the stomach and then were delivered to the kidney and liver through the blood. Moreover, The CQDs proposed exhibits a potential advantage, i.e. because on the surface of it, there are many amino and carboxyl thus it can be easily achieved by surface modification to realize the multiple functional, such as to further improve performance and achieve target.

In summary, we have developed a one-step route to prepare highly fluorescent QCDs using citric acid monohydrate as a carbon source and Diethylene Glycol Bis (3-aminopropyl) Ether as a surface modification. The synthesized QCDs are mono disperse with narrow size distribution. Moreover, the QCDs exhibit excellent stability such that their PL intensity did not change even under storage after six months. The QCDs can be used in cells and tissues imaging without genetic disruption. To sum up, this method has significant advantages such as being simple, scalable, green, and economical, which makes it promising for real applications.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The project is supported by National Natural Science Foundation of China (60976004, 61076070) and Shanghai Municipal Commission for Science and Technology (11JC1403800).
